_metadata.description: Meniscus contaminant reference database. Every contaminant in SDWIS/UCMR5 that consumers are likely to encounter, with health, regulatory, and filtration data. MUST be reviewed by licensed toxicologist before production use for health claims.
sources: EPA National Primary Drinking Water Regulations
_metadata.disclaimer: This database represents publicly available regulatory and health information. Not medical advice. Do not deploy consumer-facing health claims without independent toxicology review.

disinfection_byproduct.description: Formed when chlorine or chloramine reacts with organic matter in source water.

heavy_metal.description: Metals that can leach from pipes, industrial discharge, or natural mineral deposits.

pfas.description: Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances used in nonstick, stain-resistant, and firefighting products. Persistent and bioaccumulative.

radionuclide.description: Naturally occurring or industrial radioactive substances.

microbiological.description: Bacteria, viruses, and protozoa. Usually addressed by disinfection at the utility.

pesticide.description: Agricultural chemical runoff into source water.

voc.description: Industrial solvents and fuel components that can enter water via spills or leaks.

inorganic.description: Non-metallic chemicals including fluoride, nitrate, and common ions.

contaminants.name: Total Trihalomethanes
typical_sources: Chlorination of organic matter in source water
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Long-term exposure is associated with increased bladder cancer risk and may affect fetal development.
contaminants.regulatory_group: Stage 2 Disinfectants and Disinfection Byproducts Rule

contaminants.name: Chloroform
typical_sources: Chlorine disinfection of water containing organic matter
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen; linked to liver, kidney, and central nervous system effects.

contaminants.name: Bromoform
typical_sources: Chlorine disinfection of water containing bromide
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen; associated with liver effects and fetal development concerns.

contaminants.name: Bromodichloromethane
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen; linked to bladder cancer and adverse pregnancy outcomes.

contaminants.name: Dibromochloromethane
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Possible human carcinogen; may affect liver, kidneys, and nervous system.

contaminants.name: Haloacetic Acids (5)
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Group includes probable carcinogens; associated with increased cancer risk and pregnancy complications.

contaminants.name: Bromochloroacetic acid
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Increases cancer risk; may affect pregnancy.

contaminants.name: Arsenic
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Known human carcinogen. Long-term exposure linked to skin, bladder, and lung cancers, cardiovascular disease, and developmental effects.

contaminants.name: Lead
contaminants.health_effects_plain: No safe level for children. Affects brain development, IQ, behavior, and kidney function. In adults, linked to hypertension and cardiovascular disease.

contaminants.name: Chromium (hexavalent)
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Known human carcinogen when inhaled; ingested exposure linked to stomach cancer in animal studies.

contaminants.name: Copper
contaminants.health_effects_plain: At high levels causes gastrointestinal distress. Long-term exposure can damage liver and kidneys.

contaminants.name: Mercury (inorganic)
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Damages kidneys and nervous system. Particularly harmful to fetal development.

contaminants.name: Cadmium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen. Damages kidneys; associated with bone disease with long-term exposure.

contaminants.name: Uranium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Kidney damage and increased cancer risk from both chemical toxicity and radioactivity.

contaminants.name: Radium (combined 226/228)
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Known human carcinogen; radioactive decay damages bones and increases cancer risk.

contaminants.name: Radon
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Known human carcinogen; second leading cause of lung cancer after smoking. Primarily a risk via inhalation during showering.

contaminants.name: Gross Alpha
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Composite measure of alpha-emitting radionuclides; increased cancer risk with long-term exposure.

contaminants.name: PFOA
contaminants.health_effects_plain: IARC Group 1 known human carcinogen. Linked to kidney and testicular cancer, thyroid disease, high cholesterol, pregnancy complications, and reduced vaccine response.

contaminants.name: PFOS
contaminants.health_effects_plain: IARC Group 2B possible carcinogen. Linked to immune suppression, thyroid disease, cardiovascular disease, and developmental effects.

contaminants.name: PFHxS
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Thyroid effects; immune and reproductive concerns. Part of EPA's regulated Hazard Index.

contaminants.name: PFNA
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Linked to liver effects and developmental toxicity. Part of EPA's regulated Hazard Index.

contaminants.name: HFPO-DA (GenX)
typical_sources: Replacement for PFOA in industrial applications
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Linked to liver, kidney, and immune effects. Part of EPA's regulated Hazard Index.

contaminants.name: PFBS
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Thyroid and developmental effects. Part of EPA's regulated Hazard Index.

contaminants.name: PFBA
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Associated with thyroid and developmental effects; shorter half-life than PFOA/PFOS.

contaminants.name: PFHxA
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Emerging concern; associated with developmental effects.

contaminants.name: PFPeA
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Emerging concern; limited toxicology data.

contaminants.name: Nitrate
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Acute risk of 'blue baby syndrome' (methemoglobinemia) in infants. Long-term exposure linked to colorectal cancer and thyroid disease.

contaminants.name: Nitrite
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Same acute infant risk as nitrate. Associated with cancer and pregnancy complications.

contaminants.name: Fluoride
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Above 4 ppm causes skeletal fluorosis; above 2 ppm dental fluorosis in children. At regulated levels, protective for dental health.

contaminants.name: Barium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: High levels cause increased blood pressure; long-term exposure may affect kidneys.

contaminants.name: Beryllium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen; damages bones and lungs.

contaminants.name: Cyanide
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Acute exposure affects thyroid and nervous system; usually not at levels of concern in drinking water.

contaminants.name: Selenium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Essential nutrient at low levels; causes hair and fingernail loss, circulatory problems at high levels.

contaminants.name: Antimony
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Affects blood cholesterol and blood sugar with long-term exposure.

contaminants.name: Thallium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Affects hair loss, blood changes, liver, kidney, and nervous system.

contaminants.name: Atrazine
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Endocrine disruptor; associated with birth defects, menstrual problems, and cancer in animal studies.

contaminants.name: Simazine
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Weight loss, thyroid and kidney changes in chronic exposure studies.

contaminants.name: Glyphosate
contaminants.health_effects_plain: IARC classified as probable human carcinogen (disputed). Kidney and reproductive effects at high doses.

contaminants.name: 2,4-D
typical_sources: Herbicide runoff from lawns and agriculture
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Possible human carcinogen; affects kidneys, liver, and adrenal glands.

contaminants.name: Dichlorvos
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen; cholinesterase inhibitor affecting nervous system.

contaminants.name: Benzene
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Known human carcinogen; causes leukemia. Affects blood-forming tissues.

contaminants.name: Trichloroethylene (TCE)
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Known human carcinogen; linked to kidney cancer, non-Hodgkin lymphoma, and liver effects.

contaminants.name: Tetrachloroethylene (PCE)
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen; linked to bladder cancer and multiple myeloma.

contaminants.name: Vinyl Chloride
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Known human carcinogen; causes liver cancer.

contaminants.name: 1,1-Dichloroethylene
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Liver damage with chronic exposure; possible carcinogen.

contaminants.name: Carbon Tetrachloride
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen; causes liver damage.

contaminants.name: Xylenes (total)
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Nervous system effects at high exposure.

contaminants.name: Ethylbenzene
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Possible human carcinogen; affects liver and kidneys.

contaminants.name: Toluene
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Nervous system, kidney, and liver effects at high exposure.

contaminants.name: 1,2-Dichloroethane
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Probable human carcinogen.

contaminants.name: Total Coliform Bacteria
contaminants.epa_mcl_description: No more than 5% of monthly samples positive
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Indicator organisms; presence suggests potential contamination by fecal matter or breached treatment.

contaminants.name: E. coli
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Indicates recent fecal contamination; can cause severe gastrointestinal illness, especially in vulnerable populations.

contaminants.name: Cryptosporidium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Causes cryptosporidiosis, severe diarrheal illness. Particularly dangerous for immunocompromised individuals. Chlorine-resistant.

contaminants.name: Giardia
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Causes giardiasis, prolonged gastrointestinal illness.

contaminants.name: Lithium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Emerging concern; monitored under UCMR5. Therapeutic at controlled doses; chronic exposure may affect thyroid and kidney function.

contaminants.name: Manganese
contaminants.health_effects_plain: At high chronic exposure, linked to neurological effects especially in infants.

contaminants.name: Iron
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Secondary standard (aesthetic); causes staining and metallic taste. Essential nutrient at moderate levels.

contaminants.name: Hardness (as CaCO3)
typical_sources: Calcium and magnesium from natural geology
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Not a health concern; causes scale buildup in plumbing and reduces soap effectiveness. Very hard water (>180 mg/L) can affect appliance lifespan.

contaminants.name: Sodium
contaminants.health_effects_plain: At high levels may contribute to hypertension concerns for sodium-restricted diets.

contaminants.name: Total Dissolved Solids
typical_sources: Combined minerals and salts from source water and treatment
contaminants.health_effects_plain: Aesthetic measure affecting taste. High TDS doesn't inherently indicate contamination.
